title: Signatures of the exciton gas phase and its condensation in monolayer 1T-ZrTe2

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies monolayer 1T-ZrTe2 grown by MBE on bilayer graphene and combines STM, ARPES, trARPES, and DFT to argue that its charge-density-wave transition is preceded by an intermediate high-temperature correlated state consistent with a preformed exciton gas. The key finding is a two-step band-folding process: one valence-band folding persists above the CDW transition and is attributed to excitonic hybridization, while a second folding appears only in the low-temperature CDW state, consistent with condensation into a 2×2 ordered phase.
measurement_types: STM atomically resolved imaging / FFT of superlattice

figures.description: Schematic crystal structure of monolayer 1T-ZrTe2 in top and side views.
RHEED patterns of the graphene substrate and the sub-monolayer 1T-ZrTe2 film, showing epitaxial alignment and hexagonal symmetry.
LEED pattern after film growth on graphene, confirming surface symmetry and orientation.
Room-temperature STM large-area image showing monolayer islands of 1T-ZrTe2 on BLG substrate.
Atomically resolved low-temperature STM image showing a 2×2 CDW superstructure, with FFT inset.
ARPES band structure along Γ-M at 15 K showing folded bands and CDW-related spectral weight transfer.
Temperature series of STM images and corresponding FFTs from 4.5 K to 300 K showing melting of the 2×2 CDW order.
ARPES band structures at 14, 120, and 300 K showing temperature evolution of folded valence bands and bandgap.
EDCs at M/Γ* as a function of temperature, showing peak evolution of conduction and folded valence bands.
Temperature dependence of bandgap size, folded second valence-band intensity, and normalized 2×2 superlattice peak intensity.
ARPES band structure at 15 K along Γ-M showing the low-temperature CDW state.
ARPES band structure at 300 K along Γ-M showing the high-temperature state.
ARPES band structure of K-doped sample showing suppression of CDW and recovery of the noninteracting-like band dispersion.
EDCs corresponding to the ARPES cuts in Fig3a,c,e.
trARPES snapshots before and after optical pumping at selected delays, showing transient band renormalization.
MDCs extracted from the trARPES data in Fig3g.
Comparison of first valence-band position versus temperature/doping/pumping conditions, aligned for comparison.
Normalized spectral weight of the first valence band at Γ under different conditions.
Schematic band diagram summarizing the two-step CDW formation process.
Calculated band structures comparing PLD-driven folding and excitonic-interaction-driven folding.
Schematic illustration of PLD versus excitonic effect.
Evolution of spectral-weight transfer indicators versus carrier doping amount.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from STM topography images, atomically resolved STM scans, RHEED and LEED diffraction patterns, ARPES spectra, and trARPES pump-probe spectra. The methods specify in situ acquisition with STM, Scienta R4000/DA30 analyzers for ARPES, and pump-probe trARPES with 1.5 eV pump and 6.0 eV probe pulses.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper includes processed/derived quantities such as FFTs of STM images, EDCs and MDCs extracted from ARPES/trARPES, normalized spectral weights, bandgap sizes, peak intensities, temperature-dependent and doping-dependent trend plots, and theory-derived band structures from DFT/Wannier tight-binding calculations.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'All the data that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.'
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of measurement modalities and instruments in the Methods: MBE growth monitored by RHEED; STM images acquired in situ and at low temperature; ARPES measured at ALS and SSRF; trARPES measured at Stanford. No file formats, repository, or explicit raw-data archive are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include FFTs of STM images, EDCs/MDCs, extracted band positions, bandgap sizes, normalized spectral weights, intensity-vs-temperature plots, and carrier-doping dependence plots. The paper also describes DFT-relaxed structures and Wannier-downfolded tight-binding band structures as theoretical processed outputs.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific source data are not explicitly mentioned. The paper does not state that source data files are deposited or that figure data are provided separately.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the paper text provided.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper does not provide a public data repository, explicit source-data files, or scripts. Data are said to be available from the corresponding author on request, so replotting from the paper alone is not directly possible.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is multi-modal and figure-centric, but the text only confirms availability on request. The strongest evidence is for raw measurement acquisition and derived figure quantities, while explicit source-data packaging remains unknown.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods describe direct acquisition of STM, ARPES, trARPES, RHEED, and LEED measurements, indicating underlying raw experimental data exist.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: No explicit statement about deposited source data files, supplementary data tables, or figure-source archives appears in the provided text.
data_availability_section_relevant: All the data that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
Supplementary information The online version contains supplementary material
Methods: Film growth and STM measurements
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to infer public source-data availability from the existence of figures. The paper clearly contains raw measurements and derived plots, but only says data are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request; no scripts or repository are mentioned.
